architecture_summary: Medical Transformer (MedT) uses a gated position-sensitive axial attention mechanism as its basic building block and a Local-Global (LoGo) training strategy with a global branch on the full image and a local branch on patches. The model is designed for medical image segmentation and does not require pre-training on large-scale datasets.

novel_contributions: a gated position-sensitive axial attention mechanism that works well even on smaller datasets
Local-Global (LoGo) training methodology for transformers which is effective
Medical Transformer (MedT) which is built upon the above two concepts proposed specifically for medical image segmentation

key_claims.claim: The paper proposes a gated position-sensitive axial attention mechanism that introduces four learnable gates to control the influence of positional embeddings on key, query, and value, making the attention module more suitable for small medical datasets.
The proposed Local-Global (LoGo) training strategy combines a shallow global branch on the full image with a deep local branch on patches to improve segmentation performance.
MedT achieves better performance than convolutional and other related transformer-based architectures on three medical image segmentation datasets.
MedT does not require pre-training on large-scale datasets.
On Brain US, GlaS, and MoNuSeg, MedT obtains the best F1 and IoU scores among the compared methods in Table 1.
The supplementary material reports that MedT has 1.4 M parameters and that the gated axial attention block adds only 4 more learnable parameters to the layer.
